questions.question: What is the difference between padding and margin?
options: Padding is outside border, margin is inside
Padding is inside border, margin is outside
Padding is for inline elements only
questions.explanation: Padding is space inside the border (between content and border); margin is space outside the border.

questions.question: What does padding: 15px 30px; mean?
questions.explanation: With 2 values in padding shorthand: first = top/bottom, second = left/right.

questions.question: What happens to total width when you add padding to an element with width: 200px?
options: Total width increases by padding on both sides
questions.explanation: With default box-sizing: content-box, padding adds to the element's total width.

questions.question: How do you set padding on all four sides individually using shorthand?
questions.explanation: The 4-value padding shorthand follows clockwise order: top, right, bottom, left.

questions.question: Does padding show the element's background color?
options: Yes, padding area always shows the element's background
Only if background-clip is set to padding-box
questions.explanation: The padding area is part of the element and displays its background color, unlike margins which are transparent.

questions.question: What is padding: 10px; equivalent to?
questions.explanation: A single value applies equally to all four sides: top, right, bottom, and left.

questions.question: Can padding be negative like margins?
questions.explanation: Unlike margins, padding cannot have negative values. It must always be zero or positive.

questions.question: How is percentage padding calculated?
options: Relative to the containing block's width
questions.explanation: Percentage padding is always relative to the containing block's width, even for top and bottom padding.

questions.question: Which box-sizing value makes padding included in the specified width?
questions.explanation: box-sizing: border-box includes padding (and border) in the specified width, so padding doesn't increase total size.

questions.question: How does padding affect inline elements?
options: Padding is completely ignored on inline elements
All padding directions work, but may overlap with other content
Padding on inline elements is doubled
questions.explanation: Inline elements accept padding in all directions, but top/bottom padding may overlap with adjacent content since inline elements don't affect line height.

questions.question: What is padding shorthand for: top=5px, right=10px, bottom=15px, left=20px?
questions.explanation: The 4-value padding shorthand follows clockwise TRBL order: top, right, bottom, left.

questions.question: What is a key accessibility benefit of adequate padding?
options: It increases touch target size for better usability
questions.explanation: Adequate padding on interactive elements ensures touch targets meet the 44x44px accessibility recommendation.

questions.question: What does padding: 0 do?
options: Removes all padding from the element
Sets padding to default browser value
questions.explanation: padding: 0 removes all padding, which is useful for resetting default padding on elements like lists.

questions.question: How does padding affect an element with box-sizing: border-box?
options: Padding reduces the content area but keeps total width the same
questions.explanation: With border-box, total width stays fixed. Padding reduces the content area to accommodate the specified dimensions.

questions.question: What is the default padding value for most HTML elements?
questions.explanation: The default padding in CSS is 0 for most elements, though some elements like lists and body have browser-default padding.

questions.question: Which elements typically have default padding from browsers?
questions.explanation: Browsers typically apply default padding to the body, lists (ul/ol), and other specific elements.

questions.question: What does padding: 5px 10px 15px; mean?
questions.explanation: With 3 values: top = 5px, left/right = 10px, bottom = 15px.

questions.question: How does padding affect an element's clickable area?
questions.explanation: Padding expands the element's box, increasing the clickable/tappable area for interactive elements.

questions.question: What is a common use of padding in button styling?
options: Creating comfortable internal spacing around the button text
Centering the button on the page
questions.explanation: Padding in buttons creates comfortable space between the text and the button edge, improving appearance and touch target size.

questions.question: How do you set only left padding to 20px?
questions.explanation: padding-left sets padding on only the left side. Individual side properties are padding-top, padding-right, padding-bottom, and padding-left.
